channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Beachburg Fair Enhances Security Measures After Late-Night Incidents
item.description: The Beachburg Fair in Ottawa has implemented additional security measures following reports of late-night incidents that raised safety concerns among attendees. The decision comes after authorities no...

item.title: Kuria Backs Kang'ata in Ol Kalou By-Election, Highlights Gachagua's Fears
item.description: Former Trade Cabinet Secretary Moses Kuria has publicly supported Murang'a Governor Irungu Kang'ata ahead of the Linda Mwananchi rally in Nyahururu. Kuria warned against a 'monolithic dictatorship' in...

item.title: Senator Lacson Urges Early Resolution of Voting Threshold in Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: Senator-judge Panfilo Lacson expressed hope that the voting threshold required to convict Vice President Sara Duterte in her impeachment trial would be resolved early to prevent complications later. H...

item.title: Western Bulldogs overcome West Coast Eagles in thrilling AFL clash
item.description: The Western Bulldogs secured a 13.8 (86) to 9.16 (70) victory over the West Coast Eagles at Marvel Stadium, overcoming a spirited performance from the Eagles. Marcus Bontempelli led the charge with 25...

item.title: Modern Retro-Design Home Combines Efficiency and Style in O'Connor
item.description: The Mellow Yellow House, located at 183a Miller Street in O'Connor, is a refined mid-century modern property that seamlessly blends retro aesthetics with contemporary energy-efficient features. This t...

item.title: Kawan Restaurant Review: A Disappointing Experience in London's Chinatown
item.description: This review of Kawan, a new restaurant in London's Chinatown, highlights its failed concept and poor execution. The establishment, founded by comedian Nigel Ng (known as Uncle Roger), relies heavily o...

item.title: BMF Leadership Crisis Intensifies with Suspension of Eighth Board Member
item.description: The Black Management Forum (BMF) faces escalating internal turmoil as President Mpho Motsei suspends the managing director, Monde Ndlovu, amid allegations of misconduct. This follows the formal suspen...

item.title: Climate change threatens South Africa's food processing industry – study highlights adaptation needs
item.description: A study in South Africa's Gauteng province reveals how climate shocks are disrupting the agri-processing sector, which contributes 25% of national manufacturing output and supports over 300,000 jobs. ...

item.title: Warriors NAIDOC Basketball Tournament Celebrates Cultural Unity and Community Spirit
item.description: The Warriors NAIDOC basketball tournament in Canberra brought together over 200 teams, showcasing a vibrant celebration of cultural diversity and community spirit. The event, held across multiple venu...

item.title: Zanzibar Sees 3.1% Rise in Tourist Arrivals
item.description: Zanzibar’s tourism sector is growing rapidly, with 69,605 visitors arriving in June 2026, a 3.1% increase from June 2025. This marks a significant 73.4% monthly jump compared to May 2026’s 40,151 arri...

item.title: Conor McGregor's UFC 329 Fight Ends Prematurely Due to Knee Injury
item.description: Conor McGregor, the controversial former UFC champion, made his long-awaited return to the Octagon at UFC 329 in Las Vegas, but his fight against Max Holloway was cut short after an apparent knee inju...

item.title: Tragic Death of Andrés Escobar After 1994 World Cup Own Goal
item.description: The 1994 World Cup marked a dark chapter for Colombia, as Andrés Escobar's own goal against the United States became a catalyst for his tragic murder. Colombia, already grappling with internal turmoil...

item.title: Francesca Albanese's Critique of Global Power Structures and International Law
item.description: Francesca Albanese, United Nations Special Rapporteur on the occupied Palestinian territories, discusses her book 'When the World Sleeps' in an interview with Chris Hedges. The book explores the syste...

item.title: Free Summer Activities in Ottawa for Residents
item.description: The article highlights a list of free summer activities available in Ottawa, published on July 12, 2026. It begins with a focus on local attractions and events, such as parks, museums, and cultural fe...

item.title: Gauteng Transport Department Urges Taxi Operators to Collect Licences by August 14 to Avoid Cancellation
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Roads and Transport has issued a warning to public transport operators, particularly minibus taxi and scholar transport services, urging them to collect their operating licen...

item.title: Sandra Dacha Emphasizes Faith and Friendship Through Prayer Circle
item.description: Kenyan actress Sandra Dacha shared a heartfelt video of her prayer gathering with close friends, highlighting the importance of spiritual connections. The clip, posted on Instagram, shows women in a w...

item.title: Zanzibar's Political Accord Promotes Peace and Prosperity
item.description: A landmark political reconciliation agreement between Tanzania’s ruling Chama Cha Mapinduzi (CCM) and opposition party ACT Wazalendo in Zanzibar has been hailed as a turning point for the semi-autonom...

item.title: B.C. Sees Sharp Rise in Drowning Deaths, Safety Groups Urge Life Jacket Use
item.description: British Columbia has recorded the sharpest increase in drowning deaths this year, with at least 30 fatalities reported in 2026 compared to 20 in the same period in 2025, representing a 50% rise. The B...

item.title: Manipur farmer killed by armed Naga group; police probe ongoing
item.description: A 53-year-old farmer, Haolal Singsit, was killed by unidentified armed individuals suspected to be from an armed Naga group in Manipur's Govanjang village on Saturday. The incident occurred around 5:3...

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham Dies at 71 After Sudden Illness
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, a prominent Republican senator from South Carolina, passed away at the age of 71 following a sudden illness. The announcement was made by his office, which described the cause of ...
